frozen_components: embedding function (internal model activations before final MLP)
architecture.summary: MEGa injects event memories sequentially into a pretrained LLM (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) by fine-tuning dedicated low-rank LoRA adapters, one per memory. Each memory's embedding (derived from the model's internal activations) is stored as a context key. At inference, a gating mechanism computes softmax-weighted activations over all LoRA adapters by matching the query embedding to stored context keys, enabling the model to recall full memories and answer questions by blending relevant adapter weights into the base model.
trained_components: LoRA adapters (one per memory, low-rank weight matrices applied to MLP layers)
context keys (stored embeddings of training samples)

biblio.title: Memorization and Knowledge Injection in Gated LLMs

claims.statement: MEGa achieves a recall cosine similarity of 0.901 on the Fictional Character dataset and 0.921 on the Wikipedia dataset, outperforming all continual learning baselines
MEGa achieves QA accuracy of 72.53% on the Fictional Character dataset and 78.03% on the Wiki dataset, significantly outperforming other continual learning fine-tuning approaches

claims.dataset: MMLU (subset: abstract algebra, anatomy, astronomy, business ethics)
claims.statement: MEGa maintains MMLU accuracy at 61.75% on Fictional Character and 61.99% on Wiki Events after continual fine-tuning, matching the base model, whereas other continual learning methods exhibit noticeable decline
Internal RAG (iRAG) achieves 80.67% QA accuracy on Fictional Character dataset and 84.70% on Wiki Events dataset, narrowing the gap with external RAG
MEGa correctly selects the gating mechanism (matching query to highest-similarity context key) 85.0% of the time on Fictional Character dataset and 87.8% on Wiki Events dataset

claims.dataset: Fictional Character and Wikipedia 2024 Events (500 compositional questions)
claims.statement: On compositional questions (requiring information from two distinct samples), MEGa achieves 49.6% accuracy on Fictional Character dataset and 70.4% on Wiki Events, outperforming continual learning baselines but slightly below batch learning (54.4% and 75.2%)

All continual learning baselines (Full, Full+L2, Full+EWC, LoRA, LoRA+L2) exhibit severe catastrophic forgetting, with recall and QA performance degrading significantly as the sequence of fine-tuning samples increases

MEGa demonstrates that knowledge embedded in separate LoRA modules can be successfully integrated through softmax-weighted gating to answer compositional questions, providing the first demonstration that such weight merging enables multi-memory reasoning
